Major drivers of MetS are insulin resistance, atherogenic dyslipidaemia, prothrombotic state, elevated glucose, elevated blood pressure (BP), pro-inflammatory state and excess energy intake and concomitant obesity(Reference Grundy2). Mounting evidence suggests that lifestyle interventions (e.g. intermittent fasting and energetic restriction(Reference Sundfør and Svendsen3) or a weight reducing diet(Reference Harvie, Wright and Pegington4)) and lifestyle modifications (e.g. physical exercise(Reference Dalle Grave, Calugi and Centis5)) can reverse metabolic risk factors.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Mounting evidence suggests that lifestyle interventions (e.g. intermittent fasting and energetic restriction(Reference Sundfør and Svendsen3) or a weight reducing diet(Reference Harvie, Wright and Pegington4)) and lifestyle modifications (e.g. physic", "During the night hours from sunset to dawn, people are allowed to eat and drink freely but may not consume any food or drink after dawn(Reference Sakr6).", "However, no published works have discussed or systematically analysed the MetS components as a cluster of factors involved in the etiopathogenesis of the syndrome.", "This systematic review and meta-analysis aimed to systematically summarise and analyse available scientific evidence and clarify the results of published studies regarding the impact of RDIF on the MetS components among non-diabetic, non-athletic, he", "F.) conducted an electronic search on the CINAHL, Cochrane, EBSCOhost, Google Scholar, ProQuest Medical, PubMed/MEDLINE, ScienceDirect, Scopus and Web of Science databases for relevant studies published from 1950 to March 2019."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Mounting evidence suggests that lifestyle {"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4859102/", "title": "Ramadan fasting is not usually associated with the risk of ... - PMC", "summary": "Abstract Over one billion Muslims worldwide fast during the month of Ramadan. Ramadan fasting brings about some changes in the daily lives of practicing Muslims, especially in their diet and sleep patterns, which are associated with the risk of cardiovascular diseases. Over the years, many original studies have made the effort to identify the possible impact of the Ramadan fast on cardiovascular diseases.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["It is also currently regaining interest as a medical practice, both as preventive and as therapy and/or simple choice of lifestyle.", "Fasting takes different forms: Complete (total abstinence from food and water), liquid only (only water/liquid is consumed), partial (low calories of about 300 kcal/day), continuous or intermittent.", "Hence, fasting during Ramadan is essentially a drastic change in the lifestyle of the devotees for a full lunar month (29 or 30 days).", "Screening process AP(population): Muslim, I (Intervention): Ramadan, C (comparison): Before and after Ramadan, O (observation) (PICO) statement was set for the screening of the extant literature: Incidences or diagnosis of CHF, MI, and stroke.", "These studies, both prospective and retrospective, reported measures on incidence or patterns of acute cardiac illness, patients' demographical and clinical status with known cardiac illness and cardiovascular risk factors."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: It is also currently regaining interest as a medical practice, both as preventive and as therapy and/or simple choice of lifestyle."}, {"url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/335927480_Impact_of_Ramadan_Diurnal_Intermittent_Fasting_on_Metabolic_Syndrome_Components_in_Healthy_Non-Athletic_Muslim_People_Aged_Over_15_Years_A_Systematic_Review_and_Meta-Analysis", "title": "(PDF) Impact of Ramadan Diurnal Intermittent Fasting on Metabolic ...", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: page appears to be paywall/captcha/shell.", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/nutrition/articles/10.3389/fnut.2020.00028/full", "title": "Recent Evidence on the Impact of Ramadan Diurnal Intermittent ...", "summary": "Abstract In this article, we reviewed recent data that examined the relationship of circadian rhythm, mealtime, and intermittent fasting with the risk of cardiometabolic dysfunction. We also examined the effect of their interactions on cardiometabolic risks. Furthermore, since major differences exists between Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting compared to other forms of experimental intermittent fasting, in this article, we further restricted the discussion to Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["PubMed and Google Scholar databases were searched using “intermittent fasting,” “time-restricted feeding,” “fasting,” “mealtime,” “circadian rhythm,” and “cardiometabolic risk,” focusing on human studies published after 2013.", "Recent evidence indicates that meal timing may influence circadian rhythm, as a result, it may also directly or indirectly impact cardiometabolic risk.", "Cardiometabolic risk denotes risk factors that increase the chances of developing vascular events or developing diabetes and obesity.", "In this review, we mainly discussed studies published after 2013; though, relevant papers published before that date were included.", "In a well-designed study that aimed to assess the acute impact of misalignment between mealtime, sleep/wake pattern, and internal circadian rhythm on some markers of cardiometabolic health, 10 adults were subjected to a 10-day forced desynchrony prot"], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: PubMed and Google Scholar databases were searched using “intermittent fasting,” “time-restricted feeding,” “fasting,” “mealtime,” “circadian"}, {"url": "https://www.globalcardiology.info/site/article/view/32?articlesBySimilarityPage=3", "title": "The impact of diurnal fasting during Ramadan on patients with ...", "summary": "See how this article has been cited at scite.ai scite shows how a scientific paper has been cited by providing the context of the citation, a classification describing whether it supports, mentions, or contrasts the cited claim, and a label indicating in which section the citation was made. The impact of diurnal fasting during Ramadan on patients with established cardiac disease: a systematic review During the month of Ramadan, Muslims abstain from eating before dawn until after sunset for a month. This study reviews most recent literature on the effect of Ramadan fasting on the Cardiac patient specifically, excluding any study done on patients with no diagnosis of Cardiac disease to help cardiologists better deal with their patients who would wish to fast during Ramadan.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["See how this article has been cited at scite.ai scite shows how a scientific paper has been cited by providing the context of the citation, a classification describing whether it supports, mentions, or contrasts the cited claim, and a label indicatin", "Most studies have shown, with a few exceptions, that Ramadan fasting has no adverse effects on incidence of cardiovascular disease or the number of hospitalizations due to cardiac disease events.", "Altmetrics Downloads Citations How to Cite This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.", "A 300-400 word deep analysis focused on findings relevant to the research question, including:
   - Key data points, statistics, and numerical findings

3. 3-5 specific facts with numbers, dates, or named entities
4."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: See how this article has been cited at scite.ai scite shows how a scientific paper has been cited by providing the context of the citation, "}]}
